scenario_scope: Real intake decision for Corben's Reflexive Router v1.2 architecture proposal and its pre-deliberative command, routing, qualification, effect, temporal-context, and trace-compilation synthesis.

cases.decision_rationale: The pre-deliberative event-to-qualified-plan boundary primarily strengthens routing, while command, planning, capability, context, effect, memory, resource, benchmark, and integrated-trace details retain their existing owners. Existing-owner integration preserves layer authority and avoids a paper-shaped duplicate chapter.
required_before_drafting: completed: normalized cross-chapter terminology and precedence
completed: created passage-reviewed argument-only mappings for each receiving owner
preserved boundary: cross-check cited external literature from primary sources before external-support use
completed: bound every chapter change to the P4/P7 evidence and non-claim plan
evidence_transition_preconditions: prospectively frozen natural and adversarial campaign
independent route, outcome, and effect evaluators
promotion_blockers: no implementation or natural benchmark result
non_claims: does not validate the proposed architecture
does not promote routing or any adjacent chapter

cases.decision_rationale: A new chapter is warranted only if observed implementations show that the joined event-to-qualified-plan interface has an artifact, invariant, authority boundary, or failure class that cannot be owned cleanly by routing plus the existing command, planning, capability, runtime, context, memory, and evidence chapters.
required_before_drafting: observe at least one distinct unowned interface or failure class
demonstrate that cross-links cannot preserve ownership without duplication
define the exact manifest position and handoffs
evidence_transition_preconditions: observed rather than merely proposed boundary
promotion_blockers: current material is already distributable across existing owners
topic breadth and paper length are not chapter criteria
no campaign has exposed an unowned boundary
non_claims: does not add or reserve a new chapter
does not imply System-0 is a literal cognitive or neurological layer

validation_boundary: Records a completed argument-only existing-chapter integration and a conditional deferred chapter reconsideration. It does not validate the paper's architecture, implementation, benchmark, novelty, security, temporal truth, effect correctness, compilation safety, transfer, publication rights, or support-state effect.
non_claims: does not publish the raw paper
does not authorize a release or external publication
